title: pouteria lasiocarpa
intro: Pouteria lasiocarpa, also known as the white capupari or white kapok tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The leaves and bark of the tree have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and antioxidant protection. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
